description: Add a 'last seen URL' fallback to the open-tab feature. When a plugin has no matching tabs and no homepage (e.g., Jira, Confluence with wildcard subdomains), the extension remembers the last URL where the plugin was active and reopens it. The URL is persisted to chrome.storage.local so it survives browser restarts.

userStories.title: Persist last-seen URL per plugin to chrome.storage.local
userStories.description: Add a LAST_SEEN_URLS_KEY constant and helper functions to read/write a per-plugin last-seen URL map in chrome.storage.local. Update the tab state notification path to persist the URL of the first ready (or unavailable) tab whenever a plugin's state is non-closed.
acceptanceCriteria: LAST_SEEN_URLS_KEY constant ('pluginLastSeenUrls') is defined in constants.ts
getLastSeenUrl(pluginName) reads a single plugin's last-seen URL from an in-memory cache backed by chrome.storage.local
setLastSeenUrl(pluginName, url) writes the URL to the in-memory cache and persists to chrome.storage.local
When a plugin transitions to ready or unavailable state (tabs exist), the URL of the first tab is persisted via setLastSeenUrl
When a plugin transitions to closed state (no tabs), the last-seen URL is NOT cleared — it persists for future use
The in-memory cache is loaded from chrome.storage.local on service worker wake (loadLastSeenUrlsFromStorage)
Unit tests cover: get/set round-trip, persistence survives in-memory clear + reload, closed state does not clear URL

userStories.title: Use last-seen URL as fallback in handleBgOpenPluginTab
userStories.description: Update the bg:openPluginTab handler to fall back to the last-seen URL when no matching tabs exist and the plugin has no homepage. Also load last-seen URLs on service worker wake.
acceptanceCriteria: When no matching tabs and no homepage, handleBgOpenPluginTab checks getLastSeenUrl for a fallback
If a last-seen URL exists, chrome.tabs.create opens it and returns { opened: true }
If no last-seen URL exists either, returns { opened: false } (unchanged behavior)
loadLastSeenUrlsFromStorage is called alongside the other restore functions on service worker wake
When the handler focuses an existing tab, it also updates the last-seen URL for that plugin

userStories.title: Update PluginCard tooltip to indicate last-seen URL availability
userStories.description: Update the side panel PluginCard to make the icon clickable when the plugin has a last-seen URL (even without homepage). The background handler already handles the fallback; the UI just needs to know that a fallback URL exists.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStatePlugin has an optional hasLastSeenUrl?: boolean field
handleBgGetFullState includes hasLastSeenUrl: true when a last-seen URL exists for the plugin
PluginCard uses hasLastSeenUrl to determine isClickable: tabCount > 0 || hasHomepage || hasLastSeenUrl
When tabState is 'closed' and hasLastSeenUrl is true (but no homepage), tooltip says 'Open {displayName} (last visited)'
When tabState is 'closed' and neither homepage nor lastSeenUrl exists, icon remains non-clickable with version tooltip

userStories.title: Update Storybook stories for last-seen URL state and add unit tests for last-seen-urls module
userStories.description: Add Storybook stories showing the closed-with-last-seen-url state, and add unit tests for the last-seen-urls helper module.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard stories include a story for closed state with hasLastSeenUrl: true (tooltip: 'Open Slack (last visited)')
PluginCard stories differentiate closed-with-homepage, closed-with-lastSeen, and closed-with-neither

Unit tests for last-seen-urls.ts cover: getLastSeenUrl returns undefined initially, setLastSeenUrl + getLastSeenUrl round-trip, setLastSeenUrl no-op when unchanged, loadLastSeenUrlsFromStorage restores cache
background-message-handlers.test.ts includes a test for handleBgOpenPluginTab falling back to last-seen URL

userStories.title: E2E test: open tab with last-seen URL fallback
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that verifies the last-seen URL fallback: open a test app tab (URL is remembered), close it, then click the plugin icon in the side panel — the last-seen URL should be opened in a new tab.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test opens a test app tab, waits for plugin to become ready, then closes the tab
After tab close, plugin shows as NOT CONNECTED in the side panel
Clicking the plugin icon opens a new tab at the previously-visited URL
The new tab URL matches the original test app URL (or at least the origin)
Test uses polling assertions (expect.poll), no fixed sleeps
